```python
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def removeZeroSumSublists(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        dummy.next = head
        prefix_sum = 0
        seen = {0: dummy}
        while head:
            prefix_sum += head.val
            if prefix_sum in seen:
                node = seen[prefix_sum]
                tmp = node.next
                tmpPrefix = prefix_sum + tmp.val
                while tmp != head:
                    del seen[tmpPrefix]
                    tmp = tmp.next
                    tmpPrefix = tmp.val + tmpPrefix
                node.next = head.next
            else:
                seen[prefix_sum] = head
            head = head.next
        return dummy.next
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses a dummy node and a hashmap to track prefix sums, allowing for efficient removal of zero-sum sublists in a single pass.

# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def removeZeroSumSublists(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        dummy.next = head
        prefix_sum = 0
        seen = {0: dummy}
        while head:
            prefix_sum += head.val
            if prefix_sum in seen:
                node = seen[prefix_sum]
                tmp = node.next
                tmpPrefix = prefix_sum + tmp.val
                while tmp != head:
                    del seen[tmpPrefix]
                    tmp = tmp.next
                    tmpPrefix = tmp.val + tmpPrefix
                node.next = head.next
            else:
                seen[prefix_sum] = head
            head = head.next
        return dummy.next
